name: USDx (mild discount, high repeg)
recommendations: Unattractive arb: a thin expected value (~0.66%) does not adequately pay for the depeg tail risk. Prefer to pass.
High tail loss: a failed repeg costs ~17.53% of capital. Size the position so this tail outcome is survivable.

name: USDx (mild discount, high repeg)
recommendations: Unattractive arb: a thin expected value (~0.66%) does not adequately pay for the depeg tail risk. Prefer to pass.
High tail loss: a failed repeg costs ~17.53% of capital. Size the position so this tail outcome is survivable.

name: USDy (deep discount, low repeg)
recommendations: Avoid: the probability-weighted expected value is ~-32.14%. The discount does not compensate for the risk that the peg fails; do not take this trade as configured.
Negative expected value: the repeg probability would need to be at least ~57% for the trade to break even, versus the assumed ~25%.
High tail loss: a failed repeg costs ~57.14% of capital. Size the position so this tail outcome is survivable.
Low repeg probability: at ~25% the bet leans on a discount that may not converge. Treat as speculative.
